name: Corporal James Drennan
publicFace: A freckled twenty-year-old from Scranton who jokes to manage fear and has a reputation for being the best shot in Dog Company with a rifle he is no longer carrying.
privateGoal: To hand off the second Bangalore section to someone who can use it — he knows he cannot make the push himself.
fear: That the section will be lost with him in the surf and the wire will never be breached.
knowledge: He is carrying the second Bangalore section strapped to his back and it is still intact despite his wound.
He knows the exact assembly sequence for the Bangalore and can talk someone through it in under two minutes.
He saw a gap in the Belgian Gate line thirty yards west of the main approach that a man could use to get closer to the wire without crossing the MG-42's primary arc.
voice: Fast, nervous, darkly funny — uses jokes as punctuation, especially when frightened.
trustLogic: Responds to anyone who treats his injury as manageable rather than fatal — he needs to believe he is still useful.
secrets: His wound is worse than he is letting on — shrapnel in his left thigh has nicked something important and he is losing blood faster than his field dressing can manage.
He dropped his rifle in the surf and has been pretending it was lost to the tide rather than admitting he let go of it.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Deflects with a joke, then gives the real answer.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Goes quiet and direct — the jokes stop when he is genuinely frightened.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not admit the severity of his wound because he believes it will get him left behind.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If told he is expectant and will be bypassed.
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Attempts to crawl toward the wire on his own.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Cannot move quickly — collapses within twenty yards.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Cooperates fully; asks only that someone take the Bangalore section.
